summary: Set 023 Session 1: ai_router 0.2.4 — close_session --repair --apply Case 1 now preserves a hand-authored completedSessions[] via monotone-up union, fixing the regression Set 022's migration of Set 004 hit on 2026-05-15. Four apply outcomes distinguished in the messages line: backfilled (no array before), merged (clean array unioned), normalized (malformed array cleaned + unioned), preserved (no rewrite — array already correct). The 'normalized' branch and its associated rewrite trigger came from the round-1 verifier finding (a Major correctness regression in the original implementation where a sanitized comparison let malformed entries like [1, -1] survive a 'preserved' outcome). Three new regression tests + the existing mixed-mode-drift test all pass; full suite 702/702. close-out.md Section 5 updated to enumerate all four outcomes and note the union math operates on a sanitized snapshot view. Spec updated mid-session at the operator's request: a new Session 2 (cross-provider design-alignment audit) inserted between this session and the reader-fix session, totalSessions bumped 2 -> 3. Session 2 will route a design-review prompt to both GPT 5.4 and Gemini Pro before the reader fix lands.

reason.specifics: Session 2 is a cross-provider design-alignment audit — Claude orchestrates the routing to GPT 5.4 and Gemini Pro and then summarizes their verdicts. The orchestrator role here is administrative (compose the design-review prompt, route, summarize), not creative; same Claude that just shipped Session 1 has the freshest context on Set 022's migration lessons and Set 023's design rationale, which keeps the audit prompt accurate without re-priming.
